items.title: Tighten AnalyticsInsight.data to InsightDataMap discriminated union
items.notes: shared/types/analytics.ts currently types AnalyticsInsight.data as Record<string, unknown>, forcing `as never` / `as unknown as` escape hatches at every write path (anomaly-detection.ts:639, outcome-tracking.ts:329, reports.ts). Narrow to InsightDataMap[InsightType] so TypeScript can verify new-insight shapes at the write site. Requires updating every call site that spreads into insight.data. Discovered during the 246→0 typecheck cleanup in PR #162.

items.title: Reconcile KeywordStrategy schema ↔ interface drift
items.notes: server/workspaces.ts uses a cast-through-unknown when loading KeywordStrategy rows because the Zod schema and the TS interface have drifted (e.g. pageMap fields, scoringConfig partial overrides). Reconcile by either: (a) generating the TS interface from the Zod schema via z.infer, or (b) adding missing fields to the interface and tightening the schema. Same pattern applies to the scoringConfig partial override in workspaces.ts. Both currently work via runtime fallbacks but bypass compile-time checks.

items.title: Reconcile PageHealthData shape drift between reports.ts and analytics schema
items.notes: Shipped — PR 3 Platform Health Sprint. Extended PageHealthData interface with optional audit-enrichment fields (auditSnapshotId, errorCount, warningCount, topIssues) and removed the `as never` cast.

items.title: Verify client_signal → 'internal' category mapping in email-throttle
items.notes: server/email-throttle.ts has a guessed mapping of the client_signal insight category to 'internal' throttle bucket. Needs human review — confirm this matches intent or adjust. items.title: Remove applyScoreAdjustment return-type escape hatches
items.notes: anomaly-detection.ts:639 and outcome-tracking.ts:329 use `data: newData as never` because applyScoreAdjustment returns Partial<AnalyticsInsight> which doesn't overlap with the narrow discriminated union of InsightDataMap. Fix by making applyScoreAdjustment generic over InsightType so it preserves the data shape through the score adjustment. Blocked on #580.

items.title: Voice/Brand-engine review follow-ups (PR #166 + PR #168 deferred items)
items.notes: Deferred findings from two back-to-back scaled code reviews on the voice-authority subsystem. Tracked together because they touch the same code paths and should be batched into a single hardening PR. ## Batch A — PR #166 review (5 items, original tracker): (A1) DONE — safeBrandEngineRead() wrapper added around getVoiceProfile() in buildSeoContext (shipped in prior PR). (A2) DONE — BrandscriptTab stale-content toast: server rejects with 409 Conflict when expectedUpdatedAt is stale; frontend catches 409 and shows toast. (A3) DONE — Removed vacuous `if (workspaceId)` guards in seo-context.ts at the cache-set and shadow-mode lines. (A4) Shadow-mode voice parity check is currently skipped when the voice profile is authoritative (see comment in seo-context.ts line ~232) — add a dedicated voice-profile parity field so shadow-mode covers the whole prompt surface. (A5) Voice profile auto-creation UX: first GET /api/voice/:id creates a draft profile as a side effect. Move this to an explicit POST on the voice calibration page so the UX is intentional rather than surprising. ## Batch B — PR #168 review (13 Important items + 15 Minor items; C1 + I1-I5 + I17 were handled in the PR #168 refactor stack, excluded here). (B1) DONE — shipped in prior PR. (B2) DONE — shipped in prior PR. Important (ship after merge): (I6) Approved deliverable reset cascade missing — approving a new deliverable should reset downstream state, currently doesn't. (I7) CalibrationVariation.ratings column is never populated — schema has it, no write path writes to it. (I8) localFeedback state in RubricFeedback is dead UI — captured but never persisted or surfaced. (I9) PATCH extractions route missing atomicity + addActivity + cache invalidation — partial write on failure, no audit trail, stale reads. (I10) PUT sections route missing addActivity — activity log has no record of section edits. (I11) SteeringChat auto-summarization missing — forward-compatibility contract from Phase 1 spec addendum; long chats will exceed token budgets without summarization. (I12) Prompt injection defense not applied to discovery ingestion rawText — untrusted blob fed into AI prompts without sanitization. (I13) Brand-engine routes missing tier gating — Free-tier workspaces can hit Growth-tier features (brandscript + voice calibration). (I14) No rate limiting on brand-engine AI routes — expensive endpoints with no per-workspace throttle. (I15) rawText has no size limit at ingest — a megabyte blob will crash prompt assembly and run up OpenAI bill. (I16) err.message leakage in error responses on brand-engine routes — raw DB errors surfaced to client. (I18) docs/rules/pr-check-rule-authoring.md has stale CHECKS-array examples — references old format that no longer matches the generated rules. (I19) docs/superpowers/plans/COPY_ENGINE_GUARDRAILS.md references `.windsurf/rules/` path + stale `tsc --noEmit` command — paths moved and command is wrong (use `npm run typecheck`). Minor: (M1-M15) 15 items covering dead imports, comment typos, type narrowing, naming, and doc polish — enumerated in the PR #168 review thread; list in-place in the follow-up PR's commit checklist rather than duplicating here.

items.title: Shared WebSocket event bus — eliminate per-hook WS connections
items.source: Platform Health Sprint PR1 Devin review (2026-04-14)
items.notes: useWorkspaceEvents() creates a new WebSocket connection per call. SeoEditor + SeoAudit (and other multi-hook components) each open dedicated WS connections, scaling linearly with component count. Fix: lift workspace WS connection to a shared React context (or use the existing event bus pattern) so all hooks share one connection per workspaceId. Not urgent — connections close on unmount, but it's wasteful and could cause scaling issues with many concurrent users.

items.title: AI Research Mode — Anti-Hallucination Prompt Blocks
items.source: USER REQUEST (Reddit find: Anthropic docs anti-hallucination techniques)
items.notes: Add toggleable 'research mode' prompt blocks to AI features that make factual claims. Three techniques from Anthropic docs: (1) Allow 'I don't know' — stops confident-sounding fiction. (2) Verify with citations — retract unsourced claims. (3) Direct quotes for factual grounding — extract verbatim before analyzing. Apply to: content briefs (competitor insights, SERP analysis), SEO audit AI recommendations, content post generation, admin chat. Do NOT apply to creative features (SEO rewrites, keyword brainstorming) — research shows citation constraints reduce creative output. Implementation: add researchMode flag to callOpenAI opts, inject prompt blocks conditionally.

items.title: E-E-A-T Asset Library — Personalized Trust Signal Recommendations
items.notes: Current E-E-A-T guidance in content briefs is generic per-pillar text ('include case studies'). Gap: no inventory of what assets a client ACTUALLY has. Build: (1) Workspace-level E-E-A-T profile in settings — admin tags existing client assets: testimonials (with quotes + attribution), case studies (with URLs + metrics), certifications/credentials, before/after galleries, team bios/expertise, awards/recognition, data/research, client logos. (2) Content briefs inject specific asset recommendations: 'Add the Dr. Smith testimonial from /reviews' or 'Include the Acme Corp case study showing 40% conversion lift'. (3) Page Intelligence flags pages missing trust signals based on page type (service pages need testimonials, product pages need reviews, etc.). (4) Schema generator uses asset library for author/credential structured data. New DB table: eeat_assets (workspace_id, type, title, url, content, metadata JSON). New UI: settings tab or workspace profile section.

items.title: Hybrid SEO provider: DataForSEO primary with SEMRush capability fallback
items.notes: When DataForSEO is selected as seoDataProvider, all keyword/domain intelligence should route through it. Capabilities DataForSEO does not natively support (backlinks, SERP feature codes) should automatically fall back to SEMRush if both providers are configured. Current state: getBacklinksProvider() already does this reactively for backlinks — DataForSEO marks the capability disabled after a 403, then falls back. What's missing: (1) Proactive capability declarations — DataForSEO registers unsupported capabilities at init rather than waiting for a failure. (2) SERP features fallback — hasSerpOpportunity() currently parses SEMRush-specific Fk codes; needs a getSerPFeaturesProvider() path so DataForSEO workspaces can still capture SERP signals via SEMRush fallback. (3) Generalize getBacklinksProvider() into getCapableProvider(capability) to handle both cases and any future gaps cleanly. (4) Workspace settings UI — show which provider is serving which data type (e.g. 'Keywords: DataForSEO | Backlinks: SEMRush (fallback)') so admins have visibility. Architecture: seo-data-provider.ts already has the capability flag system (markCapabilityDisabled / isCapabilityDisabled) — this feature expands it from reactive-only to proactive + generalized.
